black_monday.event: KOSPI tanks below 5,000 on Black Monday
black_monday.trigger: Kevin Warsh Fed Chair nomination - 'Warsh Shock'

source.title: Kospi tanks below 5,000 on 'Black Monday'
source.original_text: "Black Monday" gripped Korea as the main Kospi index crashed below the recently breached 5,000 mark and the won weakened sharply on Monday. The plunge, which gave up much of this year's world-beating gains

fed_policy.event: Kevin Warsh nominated as Fed Chair
fed_policy.implication: Potential policy shift - Warsh historically hawkish but recently dovish on rates

source.title: Kevin Warsh nominated as next Fed Chair
source.original_text: Kevin Warsh - Federal Reserve Board member from 2006-2011 - has been nominated by President Trump to succeed Jerome Powell as Fed chair. Powell's role as chair ends in May 2026, while his Board term runs through January 2028.

sectors.name: Technology/Semiconductors
key_drivers: AI semiconductors accounted for nearly 1/3 of total sales in 2025
2025 semiconductor revenue $793B, up 21% YoY (Gartner)
HBM and AI accelerator demand continues to surge
Data center capex expansion by hyperscalers
risks: US-China tech decoupling and export restrictions
Potential oversupply in non-AI chip segments
Valuation concerns after 3 consecutive winning years
Black Monday shows market vulnerability to macro shocks

sources.title: 2025 semi revenue up 21% YoY at $793bn
sources.original_text: Semiconductor revenue totaled $793 billion in 2025, an increase of 21% year-over-year (YoY), according to Gartner. 'AI semiconductors - including processors, high-bandwidth memory (HBM), and networking components accounted for nearly one-third of total sales in 2025'

sources.title: Semiconductor and beyond - Global semiconductor industry outlook 2026
sources.original_text: The semiconductor industry is undergoing rapid transformation, shaped by AI advancements, geopolitical shifts, and increased government investments in domestic production. As AI adoption accelerates, demand for high-performance chips is surging

sources.title: 2026 Trends to Watch: Semiconductors
sources.original_text: High performance computing driving foundry revenue. OEM semiconductor spending and strategic shifts. China semiconductor market.

sectors.name: Robotics/Automation
key_drivers: Global industrial robot installations reached $16.7B all-time high (IFR)
Humanoid robot installations reached 16,000 units globally in 2025
China accounts for 80%+ of humanoid robot deployments
Physical AI and autonomous robotics emerging as key trend
Robots-as-a-Service (RaaS) model gaining traction
risks: High capital expenditure requirements
Technology integration complexity
Chinese dominance in humanoid robotics
Uncertain ROI timeline for humanoid robots

sources.title: Top 5 Global Robotics Trends 2026
sources.original_text: The global market value of industrial robot installations has reached an all-time high of US$ 16.7 billion. Future demand will be driven by a number of technological innovations, market forces and new fields of business.

sources.title: Global humanoid robot installations reach 16,000 units
sources.publisher: Counterpoint Research via Robotics and Automation News
sources.original_text: The global humanoid robot market moved closer to large-scale commercialization in 2025, with installations reaching an estimated 16,000 units worldwide. China accounted for more than 80 percent of all humanoid robot installations during the year

sources.title: The physical AI craze and other automation trends to watch in 2026
sources.original_text: As companies lean further into automated software, sensor technologies and robots to fill labor gaps and remain competitive, they also are using AI tools to bolster their cybersecurity.

sectors.name: Healthcare
key_drivers: 2026 is the year of obesity pills - oral GLP-1s from Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly
GLP-1 market expected to grow $100B+ from 2025-2029 (CAGR 25.3%)
Healthcare underperformed in 2025 (12% vs S&P 16%) - catch-up potential
$36B in late-2025 M&A activity signals consolidation
Key drug launches: CagriSema (Novo), mRNA-1083 (Moderna)
risks: Eli Lilly vs Novo Nordisk incretin wars intensifying competition
Medicare/Medicaid drug pricing negotiations
FDA regulatory uncertainty under new administration
Patent cliff concerns for established drugs

sources.title: 2026 is the year of obesity pills
sources.original_text: 2026 is the year of obesity pills from Novo Nordisk, Eli Lilly

sources.title: GLP 1 Analogues Market Analysis
sources.original_text: The GLP 1 Analogues Market size is expected to grow by USD 100084.7 million from 2025-2029 expanding at a CAGR of 25.3% during the forecast period.

sources.title: 2026 Preview: Navigating Pharma's Critical Year
sources.original_text: The biopharma sector enters 2026 in qualified recovery. The stakes are high: $36bn in late-2025 M&A, looming patent cliffs, and seismic FDA shifts set the stage for a pivotal year. The incretin wars intensify as Eli Lilly eclipses Novo Nordisk to lead metaboli

sectors.name: Energy

drivers: AI data center power demand surge

key_drivers: EIA forecasts Brent crude: $69 (2025) -> $56 (2026) -> $54 (2027)
Natural gas price surge: Henry Hub $6.80/MMBtu (Jan 26, 2026)
AI/data center power demand creating new opportunities for nuclear
Renewable energy continues advance despite policy shifts
risks: Oil price decline due to abundant supply ($56/bbl Brent forecast)
Energy transition uncertainty under new US administration
OPEC+ production decisions unpredictable
Warsh Fed nomination adds macro uncertainty

traditional_energy.reason: EIA forecasts lower oil prices, abundant supply

renewables.reason: Policy uncertainty but cost improvements continue

nuclear.reason: AI data center demand, SMR progress

sources.title: Short-Term Energy Outlook
sources.original_text: U.S. energy market indicators 2025 2026 2027: Brent crude oil spot price (dollars per barrel) $69 $56 $54. Retail gasoline price (dollars per gallon) $3.10 $2.92 $2.95. U.S. crude oil production (million barrels per day) 13.6 13.6 13.3

sources.title: Energy Market Update
sources.original_text: Prompt month (Feb 2026) natural gas settled at $6.80/MMbtu, up $1.53 on Monday, January 26. Prompt month crude oil settled at $60.64/bbl

sources.title: Energy Outlook 2026: Abundant supply amid a challenging transition
sources.original_text: Oil and gas fundamentals have loosened, European carbon markets are set for a structural tightening, and low-carbon technologies continue to advance - albeit unevenly

sectors.name: Commodities
key_drivers: Industrial metals may outperform precious metals in 2026 (Bloomberg)
Copper at ~$5.75/lb ($11,000+/tonne) - AI/EV/data center demand
Gold supported by central bank purchases and geopolitical uncertainty
Multi-year commodity upcycle potential (Baker Steel)
Supply deficits across key metals
risks: Lithium oversupply concerns from expanded production
China economic slowdown impact on demand
Strong USD weighing on commodity prices
Trump tariff policies creating volatility

industrial_metals.reason: Supply deficits, AI/EV demand, infrastructure investment

precious_metals.reason: Safe haven demand, central bank buying

battery_metals.reason: EV demand strong but supply catching up

sources.title: Indices 2026 outlook: Commodities
sources.original_text: Industrial metals may outperform precious metals in 2026 as supply deficits, infrastructure investment, and post-tariff growth support copper, aluminum, and nickel prices. Geopolitical conflict, weather-driven disruptions, and rising power demand from AI and data centers reinforce commodities' role in portfolios

sources.title: Outlook 2026: Miners in the spotlight
sources.original_text: Commodities will be more important than ever in 2026 amid growing demand, structurally tight supply, and with increased strategic and geopolitical focus on critical raw materials. The mining sector enters 2026 with promise, supported by strong tailwinds for both industrial and investment demand alongside constrained supply across many metals.

sources.title: Commodities Outlook 2026
sources.original_text: We are heading into 2026 with a fairly bearish view on energy markets, while we believe most metals will be better supported by tighter fundamentals.

summary.overall_assessment: 5개 핵심 섹터 중 4개 섹터(기술/반도체, 로봇/자동화, 헬스케어, 원자재)에 대해 긍정적 전망을 유지하며, 에너지 섹터는 혼조세 예상. Black Monday(2026-02-02) 충격에도 불구하고 AI 수혜 섹터의 구조적 성장세는 지속될 것으로 판단.
key_insights: AI 반도체가 2025년 전체 반도체 매출의 1/3을 차지하며 성장 주도
GLP-1 비만 치료제 시장이 2026년 핵심 투자 테마로 부상
산업용 로봇 시장 $16.7B 사상 최고치 기록, 휴머노이드 상용화 가속
원자재는 에너지 약세 속에서도 금속류 강세 전망
Kevin Warsh Fed 의장 지명으로 단기 변동성 확대 불가피

allocation_strategy.cash_reserve: 15% (Black Monday 충격 대응 및 추가 매수 기회 활용)

summary.risk_warning: Kevin Warsh의 Fed 의장 지명으로 인한 'Warsh Shock'이 글로벌 시장에 단기 충격을 주었으며, KOSPI가 5.26% 급락하는 등 변동성이 확대되었습니다. 단기적으로 위험 관리가 필요하나, 구조적 성장 섹터에 대한 장기 투자 관점은 유효합니다.
